# Figure description: скв_131.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/drill_holes_data/скв_131.jpg`

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite borehole/well log (геологический разрез скважины / каротажная диаграмма)** — a multi-column drillhole log typical of Soviet-era mineral exploration reporting. It appears to be a continuous strip log recorded on a long, narrow sheet of paper, combining lithological description columns with geophysical well-logging curves (karotazh / каротаж). The format is consistent with standard Soviet geological survey documentation from the 1980s.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers what appears to be **a single deep borehole** (скважина), likely one of the exploration or parametric wells drilled during the 1983–1984 survey of map sheets **M-42 and N-42** in northern Kazakhstan. The log appears to run from surface to a significant depth — based on the length of the strip and typical survey scales used at this time, the borehole likely penetrates to somewhere in the range of **several hundred to over 1,000 metres**. Specific borehole number, coordinates, and depth values are present in the header and footer but are **[illegible]** at this scan resolution. Formation names and lithological unit designations appear in columns along the left side but are not resolvable at this scale.

---

## Key Content

The log is organized in the classic Soviet multi-track vertical strip format, reading from top (surface) downward. The following structural elements are visible:

### Left Side — Lithological / Descriptive Columns:
- **Narrow ruled columns** on the far left likely represent:
  - Depth scale (in metres)
  - Core recovery percentage or drilling interval notation
  - Stratigraphic/formation index (e.g., formation age symbols)
  - Lithological description column (written descriptions of rock types)
- **Irregular horizontal banding** throughout suggests alternating rock types — likely interbedded sequences of sedimentary or volcanic units common in the northern Kazakhstan Palaeozoic basement

### Central Section — Geophysical Curves:
- **One or more well-logging curves** are plotted centrally — these show **high-amplitude, irregular deflections** particularly in the upper third and lower third of the log
- Several **prominent spike-like anomalies** are visible at multiple depth intervals — these likely represent:
  - Resistivity (КС — кажущееся сопротивление) or SP (ПС — потенциал самополяризации) responses
  - Possibly gamma-ray (ГК) or neutron (НГК) logs
- **Calmer, flatter sections** in the middle portion of the log may indicate more homogeneous lithology (e.g., massive crystalline basement or thick monotonous sedimentary package)
- A **prominent vertical line** running nearly the full length of the central panel may be a reference baseline for one of the curves

### Right Side — Additional Columns:
- Additional narrow ruled columns on the right side mirror those on the left, possibly containing:
  - Mineralogical or geochemical sampling intervals
  - Casing depth markers
  - Secondary geophysical parameters
- In the **lower right quadrant**, a **boxed table or legend** is visible — this is likely a summary table (сводная таблица) containing borehole header data, equipment used, scale parameters, or stratigraphic summary. Contents are **[illegible]** at this resolution.

### Notable Anomaly Zones:
- **Upper ~20–25% of the log**: Most active and variable geophysical response — highest amplitude deflections, suggesting lithological heterogeneity, possibly near-surface sedimentary cover or weathered zone
- **Middle section**: Relatively subdued response — possible basement or thick uniform unit
- **Lower ~20% of the log**: Renewed variability in the curves with renewed spike anomalies, possibly indicating a different lithological package or mineralised zone at depth

## Text and Labels

Due to the very low resolution and faint nature of this scan, most text is **[illegible]**. The following structural text elements are identifiable by position and format:

- **Top header**: Contains what appears to be a multi-line title block — standard Soviet format would include: borehole number, survey organisation, location description, date, scale. All specific values **[illegible]**.
- **Left column headers** (top of each track): Short abbreviated labels — likely include **ПС** (SP), **КС** (resistivity), **ГК** (gamma), **НГК** (neutron-gamma), or similar standard karotazh abbreviations — **[illegible at this resolution]**
- **Depth axis**: Numerical depth values should appear along the left margin at regular intervals — individual values **[illegible]**
- **Bottom footer / summary box** (lower right): A rectangular bordered table — likely contains:
  - Масштаб глубин (depth scale)
  - Масштаб кривых (curve scale)
  - Operator/геофизик signature block
  - Possibly: *"Составил:"*, *"Проверил:"*, *"Дата:"*
  - Specific content **[illegible]**
- **Annotations along log**: Several short horizontal tick marks and possible formation boundary labels appear at irregular intervals throughout the left columns — **[illegible]**

## Research Value

This composite borehole log is a primary subsurface data record that directly documents the **lithological sequence and physical rock properties** penetrated by the drill at one specific location within the M-42/N-42 survey area, providing ground-truth calibration for the regional seismic, gravity, and magnetic surveys conducted during the same campaign. The geophysical curve anomalies — particularly the spike zones in the upper and lower sections of the log — could indicate **mineralised intervals, altered zones, or lithological contacts** of direct relevance to mineral potential assessment. A geologist or AI research assistant would use this log to correlate subsurface stratigraphy between boreholes, identify depth ranges of interest for further investigation, and validate interpretations of surface geophysical anomalies with measured subsurface data.
